info.title: Byteful Public User API
info.description: Public API used for third-party usage.

get.description:
This endpoint analyzes proxy usage for a given customer within a specified time range
and provides breakdowns by proxy users, hostnames, and network types.

You can either:

1. Provide a "preset" parameter, which automatically sets the time period
   (e.g. "last_day", "last_hour", etc.).
2. OR, manually specify your own time range using period_start and period_end.

You can also filter by hostname, network, and proxy_user_id.
Hostname filters are disallowed if searching older than 90 days.

parameters.name: preset
parameters.description: If provided, overrides period_start and period_end. Must be one of ["last_hour", "last_day", "last_week", "last_30_days", "last_60_days", "last_90_days", "last_year"].

parameters.name: period_start
parameters.description: Start date for the analytics period. If not provided, defaults to 7 days ago.

parameters.name: period_end
parameters.description: End date for the analytics period. If not provided, defaults to current time.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Filter results by specific hostname. Cannot be used for data older than 90 days.

parameters.name: network
parameters.description: Filter results by network type.

parameters.name: proxy_user_id
parameters.description: Filter results by proxy user ID. Must belong to the current customer.

parameters.name: return_proxy_users
parameters.description: Whether to include proxy user data in response. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: return_hostnames
parameters.description: Whether to include hostname data in response. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: return_networks
parameters.description: Whether to include network data in response. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort results by, with optional _asc or _desc suffix to specify direction.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return.

bytes.description: Total bytes transferred for this hostname

requests.description: Total number of requests to this hostname

bytes.description: Total bytes transferred through this network

requests.description: Total number of requests made through this network

bytes.description: Total bytes transferred by this proxy user

requests.description: Total number of requests made by this proxy user

total_bytes.description: Total number of requests in time period

total_requests.description: Total number of requests in time period

get.description:
This endpoint analyzes proxy usage for a given customer within a specified time range
and groups them by one of four discrete intervals: `minute`, `hour`, `day`, or `month`.

You can either

1. Provide a `preset` parameter, which automatically sets period and interval
   (e.g. `last_day`, `last_hour`, etc.)
2. OR, manually specify your own time range (`period_start`, `period_end`) plus `interval`

You can also filter by `hostname`, `network`, `proxy_user_id`, and `service_id`.
Service ID can only be filtered if period is within 7 days and interval is hour or less.
Hostname filters are disallowed if searching older than 90 days.

parameters.name: preset
parameters.description: If provided, overrides period_start, period_end, and interval. Must be one of ["last_hour", "last_day", "last_week", "last_30_days", "last_60_days", "last_90_days", "last_year"].

parameters.name: show_log_concurrency
parameters.description: If true, includes avg_log_concurrency and max_log_concurrency per interval bucket and total_avg_log_concurrency and total_max_log_concurrency for the period. Only valid when interval is minute or hour (raw log data). Returns 422 for day or month intervals.

intervals.description: An array of usage data grouped by the specified interval.

bytes.description: Total bytes transferred in this interval.

error.description: Number of failed requests in this interval.

interval.description: Exact timestamp (or date, if day/month) representing the interval start.

requests.description: Number of total requests in this interval.

smartpath_money_saved.description: Total money in cents saved by Smartpath routing in this interval.

smartpath_routed_bytes.description: Total bytes optimized by Smartpath in this interval.

smartpath_routed_requests.description: Total requests optimized by Smartpath in this interval.

successful.description: Number of successful requests in this interval.

total_bytes.description: The sum of all data bytes transferred.

total_error.description: The total number of failed requests.

total_requests.description: The total number of requests made.

total_smartpath_money_saved.description: Total money in cents saved by Smartpath routing.

total_successful.description: The total number of successful requests.

get.description:
This endpoint returns a simplified product list that is both visible
and enabled for API checkouts. It helps clients to quickly identify and
purchase available products without any search or pagination parameters.
Ideal for basic API-based purchasing.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the simplified checkout catalog.

country_id.description: The ISO country code for the product.

product_is_available.description: Flag indicating if the product is currently available.

product_is_per_ip.description: Specifies if the product is purchased on a per-IP basis.

product_protocol.description: Network protocol used by the product (e.g., ipv4).

data.description: Object containing available products keyed by product ID

post.description:
This endpoint creates a checkout for the authenticated user based on the provided data. It supports
different payment platforms, promotional codes, and can handle both one-time and recurring payments.
You can either specify a `product_code` directly or supply the traditional combination of
`product_type`, `country_id`, and `product_protocol`.

**Important considerations**
- If `product_code` is provided alongside `product_type`/`country_id`/`product_protocol`, their values must match the actual product attributes.
- If the invoice amount remaining is `0`, it means your available credit fully covered the cost and the service is considered immediately paid.
- If the invoice needs payment, you will be returned an `invoice_url` to complete checkout.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

requestBody.description: The data needed to create the checkout.

country_id.description: An ISO country code used for region-specific datacenter or ISP products.

cycle_interval.description: The billing interval for recurring purchases.

cycle_interval_count.description: The number of intervals for a recurring cycle (e.g. 1 month, 2 months, etc.).

product_code.description: A direct product code (e.g. "datacenter_us"). If this is provided, you do not need `product_type`, `country_id`, or `product_protocol`.

product_type.description: The type of product to purchase (if `product_code` is not used).

promotional_code.description: A promotional code to apply a discount to the checkout.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product to purchase.

service_fulfillment_filter.description: An optional filter object for advanced product/service configurations.

201.description: Successfully created a checkout. Depending on the remaining invoice amount, it may already be paid by credit, or you may need to complete payment at the returned invoice URL.

created.description: A list of service IDs created during checkout (usually contains only one).

invoice_is_paid.description: Indicates whether the invoice is already paid.

invoice_url.description: URL where the user should be redirected to complete payment (null if paid in full).

mobile_ledger_created.description: A list of mobile ledger entries created (may be empty).

proxies.description: A list of proxy objects tied to the newly created service.

ip_address_id_v6.description: IPv6 address if dual protocol is used

proxy_ip_address_v6.description: IPv6 address if dual protocol is used

subnet_id_v6.description: IPv6 subnet if dual protocol is used

proxy_edited.description: A list of proxies edited or updated in the process (may be empty).

residential_ledger_created.description: A list of residential ledger entries created (may be empty).

service.description: Detailed information about the provisioned service (null if not yet provisioned).

service_id.description: The ID of the newly created service.

message.example: Successfully created checkout and paid with credit. Your service will be provisioned shortly.

post.description:
This endpoint allows a user to generate a quote for a service via the API. You can either specify
a `product_code` directly or use the traditional combination of `product_type`, `country_id`, and
`product_protocol`.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

requestBody.description: The data needed to create the checkout quote.

country_id.description: The ISO country code of the product. Applicable for region-specific datacenter/isp products.

cycle_interval_count.description: The interval count of the product.

product_code.description:
If provided, this overrides product_type, country_id, and product_protocol.
The cycle must still be valid for this product_code.

product_type.description: The product type you would like to purchase.

promotional_code.description: Promotional code to apply to the checkout.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product you would like to purchase.

credit_only_checkout.description: Indicates if the checkout is credit only.

customer_credit_balance_applied.description: Indicates if the user's credit balance was used.

discounted.description: Indicates if a discount was applied.

invalid_reason.description: Reason for the cart being invalid (only set if is_valid=false).

is_top_up.description: Indicates if the cart is for a top-up.

is_valid.description: Indicates if the cart is valid to checkout.

top_up_amount.description: Amount intended for credit top-up (if applicable).

total.description: Total cost after discounts, before credit.

total_after_applied_credit.description: Total cost after discounts and credit.

get.description: Retrieves the profile of the currently authenticated customer, along with their credit balance.

Retrieves geolocation information for a given IP address and maps it to internal API objects including country, subdivision and ASN. The response includes both external data provided by an external geolocation provider and internal data derived from internal databases.

parameters.name: ip_address
parameters.description: IP address for which geolocation information is required

external.description: External geolocation data from external geolocation provider.

internal.description: Internal geolocation data derived from internal databases.

get.description:
This endpoint creates a list of mobile proxies based on filter parameters.
The search parameters allow the user to filter the proxies by location, session type, and format (e.g., http, socks5, socks5h).

parameters.name: list_count
parameters.description: Number of proxies to return in the list.

parameters.name: list_format
parameters.description: Desired format for the proxies (e.g., "http", "https", "socks5", "socks5h", or "standard").

parameters.name: proxy_user_id
parameters.description: ID of the proxy_user you want to use to authenticate with the proxies.

parameters.name: list_session_type
parameters.description: Session type of the mobile proxies you want returned in the search list.

parameters.name: country_id
parameters.description: ISO Country Code [(ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)](https://www.iban.com/country-codes) to generate mobile proxies in.

parameters.name: city_id
parameters.description: ID of the City to generate mobile proxies in.

parameters.name: city_alias
parameters.description: Alias of the City to generate mobile proxies in.

parameters.name: zip_code_id
parameters.description: ID of the Zip Code to generate mobile proxies in.

parameters.name: subdivision_id
parameters.description: ID of the US Subdivision to generate the mobile proxies in.

parameters.name: list_smartpath_enabled
parameters.description: Is SmartPath enabled for the mobile proxies you want returned in the search list.

parameters.name: list_session_ttl
parameters.description: The session ttl of the mobile proxies you want returned in the search list

parameters.name: list_mode
parameters.description: Desired mode for mobile network optimization

data.description: List of mobile proxies.

get.description:
This endpoint provides a detailed summary of a mobile service.
It returns information such as service details, usage statistics, and associated proxy users.

200.description: Successful retrieval of mobile service summary.

Filters are applied when parameters are present and not null.

parameters.name: city_name

parameters.name: asn_id
parameters.description: Filter by Autonomous System Number

parameters.name: zip_code_alias
parameters.description: Filter by postal/ZIP code

get.description:
Search mobile availability records with optional grouping capabilities.
Use `group_by` parameter to aggregate results by specified field(s).

parameters.name: country_id
parameters.description: Filter by 2-character country code

parameters.name: subdivision_id
parameters.description: Filter by state/province identifier

parameters.name: city_name
parameters.description: Filter by city name

parameters.name: asn_id
parameters.description: Filter by Autonomous System Number

parameters.name: zip_code_alias
parameters.description: Filter by postal/ZIP code

parameters.name: mobile_availability_node_count
parameters.description: Filter by node count

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description:
Group results by specified field(s). Multiple fields separated by commas.
When grouping is used, returns aggregated node counts for each group.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description:
Sort results by field. Add '_desc' for descending or '_asc' for ascending.
Default is descending order.

total_mobile_availability_node_count.description: Total number of nodes available for given filter parameters

post.description:
This endpoint returns a list of all active proxy users on the customer's account,
along with a boolean flag indicating whether each proxy user has access to export/use
the specified proxies based on their access type and ACL rules.

**Access Logic:**
- **Unrestricted (all)**: Proxy user has access to all proxies on the account
- **Service Restricted**: Proxy user has access only if they have ACL entries for all services
  containing the specified proxies
- **Proxy Restricted**: Proxy user has access only if they have ACL entries for all
  specified proxies

**Note:** This endpoint is intentionally a POST request (rather than GET) to support
large lists of proxy IDs in the request body.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

proxy_ids.description:
List of proxy UUIDs to check access against.
Either proxy_ids or search_filter must be provided, but not both.

search_filter.description:
Search filter object to find proxies (same format as /proxy/list_by_search endpoint).
Either proxy_ids or search_filter must be provided, but not both.

200.description: Successfully retrieved proxy user list options.

data.description: List of proxy users with access information.

has_access.description:
Indicates whether this proxy user has permission to access/export
all of the specified proxies based on their access type and ACL rules.

proxy_user_id.description: The unique identifier for the proxy user.

proxy_user_is_default.description: Indicates if this is the default proxy user for the account.

message.example: Proxy user list options successfully retrieved.

400.description: Bad request - invalid parameters or missing required fields.

message.example: Either proxy_ids or search_filter must be provided, but not both.

404.description: No proxies found matching the specified criteria.

message.example: No proxies found matching the specified criteria.

post.description:
This endpoint retrieves a list of proxies assigned to the current user (or a specified
customer) based on the proxy IDs.

This endpoint is intentionally a POST even though it only retrieves data since it needs to larger lists of UUIDs.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

list_authentication.description: Authentication type for the proxies in the returned list.

list_format.description: Desired format for displaying the proxies in the returned list.

list_protocol.description: Desired protocol for the proxies in the returned list.

proxy_ids.description: List of proxy UUIDs to retrieve.

proxy_user_id.description: ID of the proxy_user used for authentication with the proxies.

data.description: The resulting list of proxies in the requested format.

get.description:
This endpoint retrieves a list of available proxies for a user based on provided search parameters.
The search parameters allow the user to filter the proxies based on protocol, version, format, and associated service ID.
The function returns proxies in various formats suitable for different purposes.

parameters.name: list_version
parameters.description: IP version preference.

parameters.name: list_format
parameters.description: Desired format for displaying the proxies you want returned in the search.

parameters.name: proxy_user_id

parameters.name: list_authentication

parameters.name: country_id

get.description:
Retrieves a specific Proxy based on the provided ID for the current user's account, ensuring
that only proxies associated with the customer services and in use are returned.

parameters.name: proxy_id

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new proxy user.

The `proxy_user_id` and `proxy_user_password` can be provided or, if omitted, will be randomly generated.

Additional considerations when creating a Proxy User:
- `proxy_user_access_type`: Controls access restrictions ("all", "service_restricted", "proxy_restricted")
- `proxy_user_is_strict_security`: If true, the Proxy User requires an IP address authentication list.
- `proxy_user_enforce_https`: If true, this Proxy User can only access targets on port 443 (HTTPS).
- `ip_address_authentications` must be a list of valid IP addresses. Each IP address can only be used by one Proxy User.
- `proxy_user_residential_bytes_limit`: An integer limiting residential traffic (must not be negative).
- `proxy_user_mobile_bytes_limit`: An integer limiting mobile traffic (must not be negative).

For access control, use the ProxyUserAcl endpoints to grant specific service or proxy access when using "service_restricted" or "proxy_restricted" access types.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

requestBody.description: The details of the proxy user to be created.

ip_address_authentications.description: A list of IP addresses for Proxy User authentication. Each IP can only be used by one Proxy User.

proxy_user_access_type.description:
Access control type for the proxy user:
- "all": Unrestricted access (default)
- "service_restricted": Access restricted by ProxyUserAcl service entries
- "proxy_restricted": Access restricted by ProxyUserAcl proxy entries

proxy_user_enforce_https.description: If true, this Proxy User can only access targets on port 443 (HTTPS). This is useful for enforcing secure connections.

proxy_user_id.description: The ID of the proxy user. If not provided, it will be randomly generated.

proxy_user_is_strict_security.description: Enable strict security. If true, IP authentication is required.

proxy_user_metadata.description:
Arbitrary metadata object for the Proxy User.
Constraints
- Supports string, boolean, float and integer values.
- Non-nested JSON object
- Max 30 keys
- Each value ≤ 300 chars
- Total size ≤ 32KB
Defaults to `{}` if not provided.

proxy_user_mobile_bytes_limit.description: Mobile bytes limit for the Proxy User (must not be negative).

proxy_user_password.description: The password of the proxy user. If not provided, it will be randomly generated.

proxy_user_residential_bytes_limit.description: Residential bytes limit for the Proxy User (must not be negative).

items.description: A list containing the ID of the created Proxy User.

delete.description:
Allows a user to delete an existing proxy user associated with their account.
Proxy users that are set as account default can not be deleted,
and already deleted proxy users can not be deleted again.

parameters.name: proxy_user_id
parameters.description: ID of the proxy user record to delete.

deleted.description: List of Proxy User IDs that were successfully deleted.

message.description: Success message indicating the Proxy User was deleted.

proxy_user_acl_deleted.description: List of ProxyUserAcl IDs that were deleted during the proxy user deletion.

patch.description:
This endpoint allows a user to edit an existing Proxy User entry by providing the necessary `proxy_user_id`.

For access control, use the ProxyUserAcl endpoints to manage specific service or proxy access when using "service_restricted" or "proxy_restricted" access types.

parameters.name: proxy_user_id
parameters.description: ID of the proxy user record to edit.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

requestBody.description: The Proxy User details to be edited.

clear_proxy_user_acl.description:
When changing access_type, set to True to clear all existing ProxyUserAcl entries.
Required when changing to "all" if ACLs exist. Optional for other access_type changes.

ip_address_authentications.description: A list of valid IP addresses authenticated for this Proxy User.

proxy_user_access_type.description:
Access control type for the proxy user:
- "all": Unrestricted access
- "service_restricted": Access restricted by ProxyUserAcl service entries
- "proxy_restricted": Access restricted by ProxyUserAcl proxy entries

**Note:** Default proxy users (proxy_user_is_default=true) must always have access_type "all" and cannot be changed to restricted access types.

proxy_user_enforce_https.description: If true, this Proxy User can only access targets on port 443 (HTTPS). This is useful for enforcing secure connections.

proxy_user_is_strict_security.description: Enable or disable strict security for the Proxy User.

proxy_user_metadata.description:
Arbitrary metadata object associated with the Proxy User.
Constraints
- Supports string, boolean, float and integer values.
- Must be a non-nested JSON object.
- Maximum 30 keys.
- Each value's string representation ≤ 300 characters.
- Total size ≤ 32KB.

proxy_user_mobile_bytes_limit.description: The mobile bytes limit for the Proxy User (must be ≥ current usage).

proxy_user_password.description: The new password for the Proxy User. Must be alphanumeric, 10 to 32 characters.

proxy_user_residential_bytes_limit.description: The residential bytes limit for the Proxy User (must be ≥ current usage).

edited.description: List of Proxy User IDs that were successfully edited.

message.description: Success message indicating the Proxy User was edited.

proxy_user_acl_deleted.description: List of ProxyUserAcl IDs that were deleted during the edit operation (when access_type is set to "all").

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new Proxy User ACL entry to grant access to specific services or proxies.

- The target Proxy User must not be deleted.
- The target Proxy User must not be a default proxy user (default proxy users have access to all proxies).
- For service access: proxy_user_access_type must be `service_restricted`.
- For proxy access: proxy_user_access_type must be `proxy_restricted`.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

requestBody.description: Details of the Proxy User ACL to be created.

proxy_id.description: The proxy ID to grant access to (required if service_id is not provided).

proxy_user_id.description: The ID of the proxy user to grant access to.

service_id.description: The service ID to grant access to (required if proxy_id is not provided).

items.description: A list containing the ID of the created Proxy User ACL.

message.description: A message indicating the success of the operation.

delete.description:
Allows a user to delete an existing Proxy User ACL associated with their account.
This permanently removes the ACL record from the database.

parameters.name: proxy_user_acl_id
parameters.description: ID of the Proxy User ACL record to delete.

deleted.description: List of Proxy User ACL IDs that were successfully deleted.

message.description: Success message indicating the ACL was deleted.

get.description:
This endpoint creates a list of residential proxies based on filter parameters.
The search parameters allow the user to filter the proxies by location, session type, and format (e.g., http, socks5, socks5h).

parameters.name: list_count
parameters.description: Number of proxies to return in the list.

parameters.name: list_format
parameters.description: Desired format for the proxies (e.g., "http", "https", "socks5", "socks5h", or "standard").

parameters.name: proxy_user_id
parameters.description: ID of the proxy_user you want to use to authenticate with the proxies.

parameters.name: list_session_type
parameters.description: Session type of the residential proxies you want returned in the search list.

parameters.name: country_id
parameters.description: ISO Country Code [(ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)](https://www.iban.com/country-codes) to generate residential proxies in.

parameters.name: city_id
parameters.description: ID of the City to generate residential proxies in.

parameters.name: city_alias
parameters.description: Alias of the City to generate residential proxies in.

parameters.name: zip_code_id
parameters.description: ID of the Zip Code to generate residential proxies in.

parameters.name: subdivision_id
parameters.description: ID of the US Subdivision to generate the residential proxies in.

parameters.name: list_smartpath_enabled
parameters.description: Is SmartPath enabled for the residential proxies you want returned in the search list.

parameters.name: list_session_ttl
parameters.description: The session ttl of the residential proxies you want returned in the search list

parameters.name: list_mode
parameters.description: Desired mode for residential network optimization

data.description: List of residential proxies.

This endpoint provides a detailed summary of a residential service.
It returns information such as service details, usage statistics, and associated proxy users.

200.description: Successful retrieval of residential service summary.

delete.description:
Allows the authenticated user to cancel a specific service associated with their account.
The user provides a service_id and optionally a cancel feedback and comment.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: ID of the Service to cancel

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

cancel_comment.description: A short comment explaining the reason for cancellation. Maximum 300 characters.

cancel_feedback.description: Feedback code indicating the cancellation reason.

edited.description: List of service IDs that were successfully canceled.

message.description: Success message indicating the successful cancellation of the service.

proxy_edited.description: List of proxy IDs that were affected by the cancellation of the service.

patch.description:
Allows a user to edit the details of an existing service.
This can include changes in payment method, adjusting automatic collection,
and marking for cancellation at the end of a period.

parameters.name: service_id
parameters.description: ID of the Service to edit

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Content type for the request body as application/json.

requestBody.description: Parameters required to edit the service.

service_is_automatic_collection.description: Flag to determine if service charge should be automatic.

service_is_pending_cancellation.description: Indicates if the service should be canceled at the end of the period.

service_metadata.description:
Arbitrary metadata object associated with the service.
Constraints
  - Must be a valid, non-nested JSON object.
  - Maximum 30 keys.
  - Each value's string representation ≤ 300 characters.
  - Total size ≤ 32KB.

edited.description: List of service IDs that were successfully edited.
